description:
Advances: specled.evidence_store

specled_-7xz (evidence-sync entry tolerance) added a warnings: [%{code:, message:}] field to SpecLedEx.Evidence.Sync.run/2's result map, populated when an entry is quarantined instead of halting sync (invalid path, malformed JSON, unsupported schema_version). The ADMISSION SCOPE for specled_-7xz restricted Allowed touches to lib/specled_ex/evidence/sync.ex, lib/specled_ex/evidence/entry.ex, .spec/specs/evidence_store.spec.md, and test/specled_ex/evidence/**, so lib/mix/tasks/spec.sync.ex and lib/mix/tasks/spec.prune.ex were intentionally left untouched.

Follow-up: wire result.warnings into both tasks' CLI output, following the existing convention in lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex (record_evidence/2, around line 237), which prints Store.record/2's warning tuple via Mix.shell().error(warning.message). Sync.run/2 already returns the data; only the two mix task call sites need updating, plus test coverage in test/mix/tasks/spec_sync_task_test.exs and test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs.

Not a functional gap (sync no longer halts either way) -- purely an operator-visibility improvement so a quarantined entry does not go unnoticed by whoever runs mix spec.sync or mix spec.prune.

comments.text:
ADMISSION SCOPE (orchestrator, post-epic follow-up; lands on epic branch specled_-csg/evidence-ledger-split, base a70ed41)
Allowed touches:
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.sync.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.prune.ex
- test/mix/tasks/spec_sync_task_test.exs
- test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs
- .spec/specs/evidence_store.spec.md (verification stubs only, if needed)
Out of scope (intent): wire result.warnings into both tasks' CLI output following the existing spec.check record_evidence/2 convention (Mix.shell().error(warning.message)). No changes to Sync/Store/Entry behavior — the data path already exists; this is presentation only. Evidence still never gates: warnings must not change any exit status. Any file outside these globs must be disclosed in IMPLEMENTED.

IMPLEMENTED
Session: impl worker (delegated, specled_-6j7/surface-sync-warnings)
Log: N/A (no separate log capture configured for this delegated run)

Commit: c79a7ae
Files touched: lib/mix/tasks/spec.sync.ex, lib/mix/tasks/spec.prune.ex, test/mix/tasks/spec_sync_task_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs
Tests added:
  - Mix.Tasks.SpecSyncTaskTest "task surfaces a quarantine warning without raising or changing exit status"
  - Mix.Tasks.SpecPruneTaskTest "task surfaces a quarantine warning without raising or changing exit status"
Verification stubs flipped: none — mix_tasks.spec.md's existing tagged_tests stub (execute: true) for specled.tasks.sync_evidence / specled.tasks.prune_evidence already targets these two test files.
Verification tier: change-scoped — mix test test/mix/tasks/spec_sync_task_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs (5 tests, 0 failures); mix compile --warnings-as-errors (clean); mix spec.check --no-run-commands (status=pass errors=0 warnings=0; the one branch-level INFO finding is pre-existing on lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex from the epic baseline, not touched by this diff); mix format --check-formatted on the four touched files (clean).
Final-tier runs: 1
Deferred must requirements: none — ticket scope was presentation-only CLI wiring; no must requirement was omitted.

Summary: Wired SpecLedEx.Evidence.Sync.run/2's result.warnings into both mix spec.sync and mix spec.prune CLI output, printing each warning.message via Mix.shell().error/1, matching spec.check's record_evidence/2 convention. Warnings are purely advisory: neither task's exit status changes, verified by tests that assert :ok while asserting the quarantine warning message is present in the drained shell output. Diff against base a70ed41 touches only the four files named above.

title: Surface Sync.run/2 quarantine warnings in mix spec.sync and spec.prune output
